From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Belgian composer and professor

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8c/Ghent3.JPG/330px-Ghent3.JPG)](/wiki/File:Ghent3.JPG)

[Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"): hometown of **Lebrun** (from left: Old post office, [Saint-Nicholas Church](/wiki/Saint_Nicholas%27_Church,_Ghent "Saint Nicholas' Church, Ghent"), [Belfry](/wiki/Belfry_of_Ghent "Belfry of Ghent"), and [Saint Bavo Cathedral](/wiki/Saint_Bavo_Cathedral "Saint Bavo Cathedral")).

Composer **Paul-Henri-Joseph Lebrun**[[1]](#cite_note-ItOpera-1)
(21 April 1863 – 4 November 1920)[[2]](#cite_note-ItOpera2-2)
was a Belgian composer and professor at the [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") [Conservatory](/wiki/College_or_university_school_of_music "College or university school of music"), who won the [Belgian Prix de Rome](/wiki/Prix_de_Rome_(Belgium) "Prix de Rome (Belgium)") for music in 1891.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Life and work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paul_Lebrun&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and work")]

Paul-Henri-Joseph Lebrun was born on April 21, 1863, in [Ghent, Belgium](/wiki/Ghent,_Belgium "Ghent, Belgium"). He studied as a pupil at the [Ghent Conservatory](/wiki/Ghent_Conservatory "Ghent Conservatory"). In 1891, in his late twenties, he won the [Belgian Prix de Rome](/wiki/Prix_de_Rome_(Belgium) "Prix de Rome (Belgium)") for music, with his [cantata](/wiki/Cantata "Cantata") *Andromeda*. He also won first prize of the Belgian Academie, for a [symphony](/wiki/Symphony "Symphony").

In 1890, he had become a professor of music theory at the Ghent Conservatory and conductor of the "Orphéon" at [Cambrai](/wiki/Cambrai "Cambrai"). In 1895, Lebrun also became conductor of the "Cercle artistique" at Ghent. He was an officer of the [Legion of Honor](/wiki/Legion_of_Honor "Legion of Honor"). Works include: the opera *[La Fiancée d'Abydos](/w/index.php?title=La_Fianc%C3%A9e_d%27Abydos&action=edit&redlink=1 "La Fiancée d'Abydos (page does not exist)")* (Ghent, 1897), orchestral compositions, and choruses. Paul-Henri-Joseph Lebrun died on November 4, 1920, in [Louvain](/wiki/Leuven "Leuven") ([Leuven, Belgium](/wiki/Leuven "Leuven")).

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paul_Lebrun&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Pietro Mascagni](/wiki/Pietro_Mascagni "Pietro Mascagni") (1863–1945), composer, contemporary of Lebrun.
* [Xavier Leroux](/wiki/Xavier_Leroux "Xavier Leroux") (1863–1919), composer, contemporary of Lebrun.
* [Pierre-Auguste Renoir](/wiki/Pierre-Auguste_Renoir "Pierre-Auguste Renoir"), painter, contemporary of Lebrun.
